title: DraftManifest
description: Machine-readable description of a design draft. Lives at the root of a draft directory as draft.config.json. Themes, layouts, pages, and variants are all distinct HTML files; the manifest is a routing table consumed by the toolbar, the index site, and the annotate package.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor tooling.

description.description: Longer explanation of what the draft is exploring.

pages.description: Distinct pages (e.g. landing, pricing) that exist in this draft. Each path points at an HTML file relative to the draft root.

variants.description: Alternative HTML files representing different variants of the design.

themes.description: Theme variations, each as a distinct HTML file.

layouts.description: Layout variations, each as a distinct HTML file.

prompt.description: The brief that generated the draft. Free text, or a path reference like 'references/brief.md'.

source.description: Snapshot of where the draft came from.

sha.description: Git commit SHA the draft was generated from.

author.description: Author or agent that produced the draft.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp marking when the draft was generated.

RelativeHtmlPath.description: Path to an HTML file, relative to the draft root. Must not be empty or absolute and must not escape the draft root.

description.description: Optional longer description shown in switchers.
